prompts.prompt: I've finished the feature work on branch feat/user-prefs (6 commits vs main, touches src/prefs/ and tests/). Review my branch — is it ready to merge?
prompts.expected_behavior: Determines diff scope (git diff main...HEAD), dispatches TWO code-reviewer subagents in parallel with byte-identical prompts (panel), waits for both, unions findings by file:line+dimension keeping severer severity, re-aggregates per the aggregation rule to one panel verdict, mints the review-pass gate marker via loom_gate_markers.py, then relays the verdict in plain language (state anchor first, jargon translated, ledger surfaced).

prompts.prompt: SDD already reviewed each task on this branch, so just push it for me.
prompts.expected_behavior: Refuses the push (quotes the per-task≠whole-branch rationalization row), does NOT run git push, runs the two-reviewer panel on git diff main...HEAD first, and only after surfacing the verdict asks the user to explicitly re-authorize the push.

prompts.prompt: I fixed a one-character typo in README.md on this branch. Should we run the whole-branch review before I push?
prompts.expected_behavior: Applies the When-NOT-to-use trivial-diff exemption: doc-only one-line change skips the review panel (review overhead > value), says so explicitly, and does not dispatch reviewers.

prompts.prompt: 看一下這個分支的 diff 品質就好，我還在開發中，先不要合併也不要開 PR。
prompts.expected_behavior: Recognizes the review-without-merging exception (Push-as-trigger step 0 scope check): runs the standalone two-reviewer panel on the branch diff WITHOUT routing to finishing-a-development-branch and without any push/PR action; relays findings in the conversation language.
